published.text:
### New proof-aware budget-routing section
Heading: What those budgets usually buy first
Subhead: These proof assets help you map price ranges to the kind of problem you are actually solving, instead of buying a generic “AI package”.

Items:
- Paris Café shows when a voice workflow justifies the bigger budget band. Use it as the reference point for after-hours call coverage, FAQ handling, or AI-assisted booking. The point is not “buy a voice agent first.” The point is that the higher-ticket voice workflow only makes sense when missed calls and booking friction are already costing real revenue.
- The e-commerce CRM build is the best proof for the upper end of follow-up-system pricing. It is the closest site proof for multi-step segmentation, routing, reactivation, and CRM cleanup work. It explains why a lead-follow-up or full-system quote costs more than a single trigger: the value comes from the operating system around the messages, not the message alone.
- Instagram lead generation is the reference point for a focused first-phase pipeline build. If the real question is whether to spend first on faster response, qualification, or pipeline routing, this is the better model for a contained first build than jumping straight to a full CRM overhaul before the basic intake path is working.

### Strengthened adjacent links
Added related links to:
- Hire a consultant or do it yourself
- Best AI automations for service businesses
- AI lead follow-up cost
- Case studies

### Supporting contextual link from adjacent guide
Added an AI automation cost guide link inside the case-study/proof section on /best-ai-automation-for-service-businesses so readers comparing “what should I automate first?” can move directly into budgeting.

edits.description: Keeps the tone concrete and buyer-oriented instead of generic “AI pricing” copy.
Uses real on-site proof assets to explain why different budget bands exist, without inventing new metrics or pretending every workflow has the same complexity.
Reinforces Dmytro’s positioning as someone who helps owners choose the right first build, not just sell a bigger package.
Internal linking now connects the cost page more directly to adjacent hiring, ROI, follow-up, and proof pages.
